assertions.title: Add multiple predicates

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3100 and select neurosynth engine
steps.observed: Engine selected, predicate browser shows Relations including PeakReported and TermInStudyTFIDF

steps.action: Click 'PeakReported(0, 1, 2, 3)' in the predicate browser sidebar
steps.expected: PeakReported added to visual query builder with placeholder variables
steps.observed: PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) added to visual builder. Display: 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)'

steps.action: Click 'TermInStudyTFIDF(0, 1, 2)' in the predicate browser sidebar
steps.expected: TermInStudyTFIDF added; both predicates visible; shared variables indicate join
steps.observed: TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6) added. Display: 'Find v, v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) and TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6)'. Datalog: 'ans(v, v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3), TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6).' Both predicates are visible, but there are NO shared variable names between the two predicates - each predicate has uniquely-named variables (v,v1,v2,v3 vs v4,v5,v6), so there is no visual join indication.

evidence.network: N/A - no API calls for predicate addition

assertions.issues: Both predicates are visible in the visual builder and Datalog output is correct. However, variables are NOT shared between predicates (PeakReported uses v,v1,v2,v3 and TermInStudyTFIDF uses v4,v5,v6 - distinct non-overlapping variables). The spec requires 'shared variable names visually indicate a join', which requires that at least one variable is shared between predicates. No visual join indication exists in this implementation.

steps.action: POST /v2/suggest/neurosynth with body {"program": "ans(x) :- PeakReported(x, y, z, s)\n"}
steps.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON response containing suggestion categories
steps.observed: HTTP 200, JSON body: {"status": "ok", "data": {"Signs": ["values"], "Numbers": ["values"], "Text": ["values"], "Operators": ["values"], "Cmd_identifier": ["values"], "Functions": ["values"], "Identifier_regexp": ["values"], "Reserved words": ["values"], "Boleans": ["values"], "Expression symbols": ["values"], "Python string": ["values"], "Strings": ["values"], "functions": ["values"], "base symbols": ["values"], "query symbols": ["values"], "commands": ["values"]}}

steps.action: POST /v2/suggest/neurosynth with body {"program": "PeakReported(x, y, z, s) :- "} (partial program)
steps.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON response containing suggestion categories

evidence.network: POST /v2/suggest/neurosynth -> 200, JSON body with suggestion categories

assertions.title: Context-aware suggestions displayed

steps.expected: Suggestions panel shows clickable chips/buttons for valid next steps
steps.observed: Suggestions panel shows two sections: IDENTIFIERS (74 clickable chips including PeakReported, SelectedStudy, Study, TermInStudyTFIDF, Voxel, agg_create_region, etc.) and BASE SYMBOLS (same symbols). All are clickable buttons with class 'suggestions-chip'.

steps.expected: Suggestions panel updates to show context-aware next steps
steps.observed: Suggestions panel shows 'No suggestions available.' - the suggestions are NOT context-aware for a query with predicates already added.

steps.expected: Suggestions panel updates to show context-aware next steps based on current 2-predicate query
steps.observed: Suggestions panel still shows 'No suggestions available.'

evidence.network: Suggestions API called on initial engine selection

assertions.issues: The suggestions panel shows clickable chips in the INITIAL state (when no predicates are added), which passes the basic requirement. However, after adding one or more predicates, the suggestions panel consistently shows 'No suggestions available.' - it does not provide context-aware suggestions based on the current query state. The spec requires suggestions to be displayed 'while building a query' and to be 'based on the current query state'. The 'No suggestions available' state after adding predicates fails this requirement.

assertions.title: Click suggestion to extend query

steps.action: Select neurosynth engine (no predicates), observe suggestions panel with chips
steps.observed: Suggestions panel shows 74 clickable chips including 'PeakReported', 'Study', etc.

steps.action: Click 'PeakReported' suggestion chip in the suggestions panel
steps.expected: PeakReported added to visual query builder with proper variables; suggestions update
steps.observed: PeakReported added to query as 'PeakReported()' - with EMPTY parentheses (no variable names). Visual builder shows: 'Find where PeakReported()'. Datalog: 'ans() :- PeakReported().'. Suggestions updated to 'No suggestions available.'.

assertions.issues: Clicking a suggestion chip does add the predicate to the query and both the visual query and suggestions panel do update. However, the predicate is added WITHOUT variable names (PeakReported() instead of PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)). Compare this with clicking directly from the predicate browser sidebar which correctly adds with variable placeholders. The spec says the predicate should be added 'at the appropriate position' - the empty variables represent a functionally broken state as the resulting Datalog is 'ans() :- PeakReported()' which has no useful output columns.

frictions.description: The React dev server at port 3100 was not running when testing started. Had to start it manually with 'npm run dev' before testing could begin.
frictions.resolution: Started dev server: cd neurolang/utils/server/neurolang-sparklis && npm run dev

frictions.description: The suggestions API (POST /v2/suggest/neurosynth) with an incomplete program (ending with ':-') returns an error message in the 'data.message' field rather than suggestions, and all suggestion arrays are empty. A newline-terminated complete program is needed to get suggestions.
frictions.resolution: Used a newline-terminated complete rule for VAL-QBUILD-006 test to get a meaningful response with suggestion categories.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-QBUILD-006 - API returns valid JSON), 3 failed. VAL-QBUILD-005: both predicates visible but no shared variable names to indicate join. VAL-QBUILD-007: suggestions shown in initial empty state but 'No suggestions available' after adding predicates. VAL-QBUILD-008: clicking suggestion chip adds predicate but without variable names (empty parens), and suggestions clear after click rather than updating contextually.
